status_detail: SAME wire failure as #633/#634. reload_templates (53 envelopes incl. you_sse_v3) was necessary but NOT sufficient. POST /api/streamingSearch still terminated at TCP/proxy layer BEFORE APF can inject SSE warning envelope. request_status: (failed) -- no HTTP status; Type=xhr, Size=0.0 kB, Time=499 ms; DevTools 'XHR request failed'; Headers tab 'Provisional headers are shown'
generic_error_text: Sorry, something went wrong. Please try again later. Our team is looking into it. ... Error ID in your message: 846fa320-a4be-4f3d-a6ce-b96ff5f6473d|4e6882cc-8198-4d33-be52-4304bc98ba8b|2026-04-28T08:08:29.962Z
progress_vs_634: SAME -- identical TCP-level failure pattern; no observable change after engine reload_templates
breakthrough_status: NO breakthrough. reload_templates (53 envelopes including you_sse_v3) was necessary but not sufficient.
root_cause_hypothesis: Request still terminated at TCP/proxy layer BEFORE APF can inject SSE warning envelope. The substitution engine likely never sees the request -- something earlier in the pipeline (ai_prompt_services action mode set to 'block_tcp'/'reset' instead of 'warn'/'replace_response') is doing the TCP RST and never reaching the SSE substitution code path.
recommendation_for_dev: URGENT: verify ai_prompt_services.action mode for service 'you'. Should be 'warn' or 'replace_response' (or whatever invokes envelope substitution). If currently 'block_tcp'/'reset', that's the bug -- engine RSTs at TCP layer and never invokes you_sse_v3 envelope.
Verify you_sse_v3 envelope binding: SELECT response_template_id FROM ai_prompt_services WHERE service='you'. Should reference id=80 (you_sse_v3).
Engine log: grep [APF:you] in /var/log/etap.log for 17:06-17:10 KST 2026-04-28. Trace the action decision: did engine select 'block' or 'replace'? If 'block', where does that come from?

Confirm tcpdump on bridge egress -- as recommended in #634 -- to see if engine emits ANY HTTP/2 DATA frames or just RST/FIN. Critical for distinguishing 'engine writes nothing' vs 'engine writes bytes that client rejects'.
Once action mode is correct + tcpdump shows engine emits DATA frames, expect SUCCESS = warning renders in you.com chat bubble.
errors_observed: Provisional headers shown (no response headers arrived)
you.com generic error 'Sorry, something went wrong'
notes: Cycle 95 you SSE v3 retry post reload_templates. FAIL = same wire behavior as #633/#634. Reload was necessary but not sufficient -- need to fix action mode upstream of SSE substitution. Subagent 4m, well within 5min soft (no overage this time).
